message: The engine's in-tool `demo`/`demo-*` subcommands are a promoted standing falsification capability (engine-planning D-231): each is a demonstration carried inside a shipped tool that travels into every generated repo, and every one must be able to FAIL — it drives the real surface and returns non-zero when the behaviour is broken (a recipe that can only succeed is not evidence). This check flags any in-tool demo whose only exit is a literal success (0/None) — a print-only showcase. The fix is to give it a self-check that acts on the result it already computes and returns non-zero on a mismatch (the .engine/tools/module_coherence.py demo is the pattern), or to demote it out of the `demo` subcommand convention. It excludes the standalone demo_*.py files (a separate population, D-228) and the first-run-retired construction subcommands.
